falsification_rule_text: Not supported if, across the five cases: (a) FIRST-ORDER rent-reduction for sitting tenants is confirmed at p<0.05 (acknowledged success) AND (b) NEW-BUILD and MOBILITY SECOND-ORDER divergence fails to appear (<1.0pp deviation from synthetic control) in at least 3/5 cases AND (c) QUALITY/QUEUE/EXEMPTION THIRD-ORDER divergence fails to appear in at least 3/5 cases. If the first-order rent suppression is confirmed but the second/third-order chain does not materialise, the causal-chain claim is refuted even though the policy mechanism works on its own terms.

variables_loaded.name: stock_quality_index
variables_loaded.source: constructed: age of building stock + maintenance complaints where available; destatis:wohngebaeude_baujahr; us_census:ACS; SCB housing register

variables_missing.name: tenant_mobility_rate
variables_missing.source: constructed: share of renters moving in past 12 months; ons:LFS; US census ACS; SWE SCB migration register

variables_missing.name: airbnb_and_corporate_exemption_share
variables_missing.source: constructed: AirDNA Airbnb listings as share of rental stock + corporate-let exemption count; official registries where available (Berlin Zweckentfremdung; SF short-term rental registry)

variables_missing.name: waiting_list_length_or_queue_years
variables_missing.source: Stockholm bostadsformedlingen; NYC HPD lottery data

variables_missing.name: binding_rent_control_indicator
variables_missing.source: constructed: 1 in treated city-years (Berlin 2020-2021, SF 1994 expansion post-1995, St Paul 2021+, Stockholm continuous, NYC continuous rent-stabilised expansion years); 0 in donor cities
